content:
Overview

Shared bastions create durable lateral trust once the perimeter is breached. This write-up describes how to move beyond bastion hosts using identity-governed connectivity and revocation-safe administrative access.

Operational Scenario

Identity gates access

Peer authorization is validated out-of-band (directory/LDAP) before a data-plane tunnel becomes usable.

Stability via protocol constraints

Packet fragmentation risk is reduced by MSS clamping so cross-boundary transport stays reliable under load.

Instant revocation

Disable the user in the directory and observe that access terminates quickly as policy gates withdraw the permitted route.

Verification

Success criteria: authorized peers establish access, unauthorized peers are dropped/denied, and revocation removes usable connectivity without requiring a restart.
